name: Another Russian Tea
directions: Boil water with cloves and cinnamon rapidly for 20 minutes.
Remove from heat,  cover, and add 4 tea bags, and let "steep" for at  least 4 hours (we like to  let it steep overnight).
After "steeping" is complete, in another pan combine: 2 cups Orange juice 2 cups Pineapple juice 1 cup Lemon juice 2 cups Sugar. Heat gently, stirring to melt and mix sugar.
Drain "solids" from tea mixture.  Combine all together and heat to enjoy.

name: Black Russian
directions: Pour over ice cubes in old-fashioned cocktail glass.

name: Sangria
directions: Mix all ingredients except ginger ale; refrigerate until chilled.
Just before serving, stir in ginger ale.
Garnish with fruit, if desired.

name: Sangria Blanco
directions: Heat sugar, water and cinnamon to boiling in 1-quart saucepan; reduce heat.
Simmer, uncovered, for 5 minutes.
Cover and refrigerate at least 2 hours but no longer tha 1 week.
Remove cinnamon sticks from sugar mixture.
Mix sugar mixture, sparkling water, apple juice, orange juice and wine in large pitcher.
Gently stir in fruit and ice.
Serve with several pieces of fruit in each glass.

name: Zorbatini
directions: Prepare like a Martini.
Garnish with a green olive.

name: Russian Friendship Tea Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients well and store in an airtight container.
To use, fill a mug with boiling water and stir in 2-3 teaspoons of mix, to taste.
If all you can find is presweetened lemonade, then use the amount of dry mix needed for a 2 quart pitcher according to the package instructions and leave out the sugar.

name: Cinnamon Mint Tea
directions: Bring first 4 ingredients to a boil.
Boil 1 minute.
Stir in mint leaves.
Remove from heat and allow to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain.

name: Limoncello
directions: Place vodka and zest in a sealable jar, like a large mason type jar and allow to sit in a cool place for 4 days.
On the fifth day place the sugar and the water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Allow to cook for 5 minutes, remove from heat and allow to cool.
Add sugar solution to the vodka solution and allow to stand overnight.
Strain through a layer of cheesecloth in a conical sieve, pressing the solids firmly.
Strain again through a second layer in the same manner.
Chill and allow to stand 1 week.
Serve very cold.

name: Russian Tea
directions: Mix together and store in a jar.
Add 2 tsp of mix to 1 cup hot water.
Enjoy!

name: Russian Tea
directions: Boil water, cinnamon, and cloves about 4 to 5 minutes.
add teabags and simmer about 10 minutes.
strain and add juices and sugar.

name: Glogi ( Finnish  Mulled Wine)
directions: In a non aluminum mid-size sauce pan, heat water and sugar over medium heat until sugar is dissolved, about 1 minute.
Tie all spices and the peel into a small piece of cheesecloth for easy removal.
Add the spices and peel and wine to the sauce pan and simmer, covered, 5 minutes.
To serve: Stand one spoon in each glass, evenly divide gogli into each glass.
Add a few raisins and almonds in each, or serve separately.

name: Hot Mulled Wine
directions: Using a sharp knife, peel lemon.  Do not use a zester because the peel will be too fine.  Extract juice and set aside.
Combine peel, cloves, one cinnamon stick, sugar and 2 cups water.  Boil 5-10 minutes.
Remove spices with slotted spoon and discard.
Transfer to a crockpot, and add wine and lemon juice.  Heat on low.
Serve hot in mugs with a cinnamon stick as a stir.

name: Gluhwein - German Hot Spiced Wine
directions: Mix all ingredients together and heat in large pan.
If serving for a party, you can keep this warm in a crock pot.

name: Cardamom Tea
directions: Place all ingredients in 2 1/2 cups of water, heat, but do not boil, for half an hour and strain.
Add honey and milk. Enjoy!

name: Swedish Glogg
directions: Place wine, orange juice, orange peel, cinnamon, cloves, cardamom, cranberries, ginger and almonds in a large saucepan and cook gently 1/2 hour.
Add the port and heat thoroughly but do not boil. If mixture comes to the boil the alcohol will evaporate.
Combine sugar with 1/2 cup of the brandy in a large saucepan. Warm it gently to dissolve the sugar. Add mixture to the wine.
Just before serving strain into a clean saucepan and heat gently. Stir in remaining brandy.
Serve in large wine glasses or mugs with orange slices.

similar.name: Mellow Glogg for a Freezing Night

name: Ouzo Martini
directions: In a martini shaker, combine all ingredients with 1/2 cup of ice.
Shake and strain into 2 chilled martini glasses.
Garnish each glass with a slice of lime.

name: Spiced Iced Tea
directions: Combine water, cloves, and cinnamon stick in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil. Remove from heat.
Place tea bags in spiced water, cover, and steep for ten minutes.
In another saucepan, combine sugar, orange juice, and lemon juice. Bring to a boil.
Strain tea into orange juice mixture.
Chill, serve over ice.

name: Riviera Cocktail
directions: Shake in a cocktail shaker with ice and strain in 4.5 ounce cocktail glass.

name: Cassisco
directions: Fill 9 ounce glass with ice.
Pour brandy and creme de cassis over ice, top with club soda and stir.

name: Vanilla and Jasmine Martini
directions: Muddle 4 whole vanilla pods with Chambord and vodks in base of a shaker.
Add Jasmine tea, ice and shake vigorously.
Jasmine tea should be steeped and cooled ahead of time.
Pour into a Martini glass.
Garnish with one split vanills bean.
Note:.
With the price of the vanilla beans and Chambord this is a very expensive drink.

name: Passion Fruit Margarita
directions: Blended:.
Add all ingredients except Chambord in a blender.
Add ice to the top of the liquid.
Blend and pour into a margarita glass and drizzle the 1/2 shot of Chambord over top.
Rocks:.
Add ingredients to a ice filled shaker, shake and pour into ice filled rocks glass.

name: Lavender Herb Tea
directions: Place the herbs in a warmed teapot.
Pour in the 4 cups boiling water, cover, and steep for 3-5 minutes.
Strain and serve.
Sweeten with honey or sugar, if desired.

name: Krupnik (Fire Vodka)
directions: Combine honey with the water, vanilla, spices and the lemon peel in a large saucepan.
Bring this to a boil, cover, and simmer.
Reduce heat and let steep for several hours.
When ready to serve, add vodka and remove from heat.
Serve immediately.
Can be reheated in a saucepan on a low setting just until you see the first wisps of alcohol steam.

similar.name: Mellow Glogg for a Freezing Night
Mulled Wine That Warms Your Soul

name: Eggnog Coffee (Non-Alcoholic)
directions: Place coffee in filter in brew basket of coffee maker; sprinkle with nutmeg.
Place sugar in empty pot of coffee maker.
Add 2-1/2 cups cold water to coffee maker; brew.
When brewing is complete, stir in eggnog.
Pour into 4 cups.
Serve immediately, topped with dollops of the whipped topping.
Sprinkle lightly with additional nutmeg, if desired. Enjoy!

name: Lemongrass and Pink Peppercorn Vodka
directions: place peppercorns and lemongrass into the bottle of vodka and leave for at least 8 hours but no longer than 2 days depending on how spicy you want it.
strain into a jug and pour back into the bottle.
store in the freezer and serve in shot glasses.

name: Emerald Isle Martini
directions: Shake all ingredients (except mint sprig) with ice.
Strain into a chilled martini glass.
Garnish with a mint sprig.

name: Irish Raspberry Martini
directions: Shake all ingredients in a shaker filled with ice.
Strain into a chilled martini glass.
Garnish with a raspberry and mint sprig.

name: Katinka
directions: Shake all ingredients well with ice.
Strain into a cocktail glass heaped with shaved ice.
Garnish with lime if desired.

name: Gluhwein (Mulled Wine)
directions: Combine all the ingredients in a heavy, appropriately sized pot and simmer slowly, giving the mixture a good stir every few minutes.
Do not allow to boil – this burns off the alcohol (unless you want a non-alcoholic, mulled drink.)
Strain and scoop into glasses or mugs when ready.

name: Quick Honey Liqueur (Greitai Pagamintas Krupnikas)
directions: Combine honey with water, vanilla, spices and lemon peel in a large metal percolator.  Bring to a boil and let it perk for about 7 minutes.  Remove from heat; add vodka.
When slightly cooled, strain and pour into a sterilized quart jar or heat-proof bottle.  Can be served warm or cold.

name: Ginger Cosmopolitan
directions: Shake all well and strain into a chilled cocktail glass.
Garnish with flamed orange zest and a small piece of crystallised ginger.

name: Cassis Champagne Cocktail
directions: Place one sugar cube in each of 8 glasses, add a capful or 2 teaspoons of liqueur to each glass.
Fill each glass with well chilled champagne.
Serve immediately.

name: Bellissimo Cocktail
directions: Combine all the ingredients, with ice, in a cocktail shaker and shake well.
Double strain into a Martini glass and decorate with the passion fruit slice.

name: Roxy Special
directions: Give one good shake, add lemon slice and cherry, and serve over ice.

name: Classic Champagne Cocktail
directions: Drip the Angostura bitters onto a sugar cube and place in the bottom of a champagne flute.
Pour over the cognac and top up with champagne.

name: Malt Whiskey & Ginger Wine
directions: Pour the malt whisky and ginger wine into a saucepan. Add the cloves and cinnamon sticks.
Heat until warm, but do not allow to boil. Pour and serve at once.

name: Russian Raspberry
directions: Mix together fruit juices and sugar in a mug.
Top with boiled water & a slice of lemon.

name: Cat Cora's Greek Mojito
directions: Place a sheet of aluminum foil in your freezer so you have a spot to chill your sugar-rimmed glasses.
Pour 2 tablespoons of sugar into a small, flat saucer. Halve 1 of the limes crosswise. Cut one half into slices and set aside for garnish.

Holding a glass upside down so lime juice doesn't run down the outside, rub the cut surface of one of the limes around the glass's rim. Immediately dip the glass into the saucer of sugar to coat the rim all the way around. Gently shake off any excess sugar and carefully place the glass upright into your freezer on the sheet of foil.
Repeat with the other 3 glasses using same lime half.
Juice the remaining limes and the lemons and set aside. You should have 6 to 8 tablespoons of the lime and lemon juice mixture.

If you'd like to add fine mint ribbons to your drinks, stack 4 to 6 mint leaves per serving, roll the leaves up like a cigar, and cut across the roll, slicing the mint into fine strips. Set these aside and with your hands, tear apart 5 or 6 mint sprigs and toss them into a cocktail shaker.
Pour in the lime and lemon juice, rum, and the remaining honey. Using a wooden spoon, muddle this together, crushing the mint leaves to release all that fragrant mint oil. Cap shaker and shake well.

Remove sugar-rimmed glasses out of the freezer toss a handful of ice into each glass. Strain the drinks from the cocktail shaker into the glasses. Divide the mint ribbons, if you like, among the four glasses, and top off each glass with a splash of club soda.
Garnish each with a slice of lime and sprig of mint. Finish with a splash of Ouzo.

name: Maiori Magic
directions: Fill a highball glass with ice, add Limoncello and Lemon Juice, top with Tonic Water, stir, squeeze the Wedge of Lime over the glass and drop it into the glass.

name: Amalfi-Tini
directions: Shake with ice and serve in a martini glass. Garnish with a lemon twist.

name: Berlin Blonde
directions: Put all ingredients in a shaker.
Shake with ice, then strain in an iced cocktail glass.
Sprinkle with a little bit of cinnamon and garnish with a marasquin cherry.

name: Gluhwein (Hot Wine)
directions: Mix all ingredients in large pot.
Heat to almost boiling, but do not let it boil.
Serve hot.

name: Absolut Wonder
directions: Rim the glass in lemon juice then in chocolate grated.
Pour vodka and liqueur in a shaker cold (maybe with ice, I think it would be colder).
Shake, then strain 2 times in a cocktail glass.
Let drop a marashino cherry in the bottom of the glass.

name: Sparkling Cosmo
directions: Shake first five ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled glass; top with champagne.
Serve in a flute garnished with a flamed orange twist.

name: French Mojito
directions: Muddle mint with vodka in base of shaker; add lime juice and sugar; shake with ice and strain into an ice-filled glass.
Top with soda and stir.
Serve in a collins glass, garnished with raspberry and mint sprigs.

name: French Cafe Brulot
directions: Place the sugar, orange rind and vanilla pod in a heatproof bowl and bruise the rind with a wooden spoon.  Pour the brandy into a small saucepan, then place over low heat until heated through.  Pour over the sugar mixture, then ignite and stir constantly for 30 seconds.  Extinguish the flames with the hot coffee, then remove the orange rind and vanilla pod.  Serve in coffee cups.  Do not serve with cream or additional sugar.  This makes about 20 demitasse cups.
The Creative Cooking Course.

name: Chocolate Caramel Martini
directions: Mix ingredients in a shaker over ice.  Stir to chill.
Strain into martini glass.

name: Cuban Martini
directions: Place the amaretto, Malibu, Kahlua, vodka and ice cubes in a cocktail shaker, and shake until well combined. Strain evenly among serving glasses. Add 3 coffee beans to each glass and serve.

name: Champagne Lemon
directions: Pour into a champagne saucer, and serve.

name: Turkish Martini
directions: Add a scoop of ice to a cocktail shaker, add vodka, creme de cacao & rose water & shake vigorously.
Strain into a chilled martini glass & garnish with a cube of turkish delight.

name: White Sangria
directions: Combine sugar, apple liquor, lime, lemon, peaches and apples in a large pitcher.
Cover with 1 bottle of sauvignon blanc and chill for several hours.
To serve, spoon fruits into glasses or goblets, adding fresh raspberries in each glass. Pour wine over top of the fruit.
Top off glasses of sangria with a splash of soda water and serve.

name: Secret Smile Champagne Cocktail
directions: Combine ingredients in a champagne glass rimmed with sugar.

name: Caribbean Champagne Cocktail
directions: Combine first 3 ingredients in a champagne flute & then top up with champagne.

name: Pink Hibiscus Cocktail
directions: Shake all ingredients in a shaker with ice.

name: Cinnamon & Ouzo Iced Tea
directions: Place the cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, sugar and tea bags in a large heatproof jug. Add the boiling water and stir until sugar dissolves. Set aside for 3 minutes to infuse. Remove the tea bags and discard.
Stir in the ouzo and place in the fridge for 2 hours to chill. Place the ice cubes in serving glasses and pour over the chilled tea to serve.

name: German Bight Cocktail
directions: Shake with a few ice cubes, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.
*note: if you can't find honey liqueur, use a vanilla liqueur and 1 teaspoon of honey to the mix.

name: Moscow Mule Cocktail
directions: Mix the ingredients together with ice in a highball glass.

name: Russian
directions: Combine ingredients in mixing glass and sir well.
Strain into a cocktail glass.

name: Cranberry Kir Champagne Cocktail
directions: Combine 1st 3 ingredients in a glass, top with champagne & serve.

name: Cosmopolitan Champagne Cocktail
directions: Stir together Cointreau, cranberry Juice, lime juice, and sugar. Chill, covered, for 2 to 6 hours. To serve, divide mixture among 10 champagne flutes, then top each with champagne.

name: Lemon Ginger Infused Vodka
directions: Poke lemons with skewer or sticks to release flavor.
Slice ginger into small slices.
Place both into bottle or other appropriate container that has lid.
Fill jar with vodka and let sit for 2 weeks.
Strain and pour into decorative decanter or bottle.

name: Italian Margaritas
directions: If desired, rub the rim of 2 margarita glasses with lime and roll in coarse sugar.
Fill a shaker with ice and add ingredients (if you don't have a shaker, use a pitcher!).
Add Amaretto, Triple Sec, Tequila, and sweet and sour and shake well.
Pour into margarita glasses and garnish with lime.
Optional "Floater": Leave a bit of room in the margarita glass and slowly pour 1/2 ounce of your favorite liqueur on top of the margarita.

name: Pear Martini
directions: Combine ingredients in a shaker over ice.
Shake, and strain into a martini glass.
Garnish with a lime wheel (or pear slice if available).

name: Mellow Glogg for a Freezing Night
directions: 1/2 bottle of red wine, all vodka and brandy and all spices, lemon, figs  heated to boil and left overnight.  This forms the base.
Next day, heat and add sugar and further 5 cardamon seeds and 5 cloves.
Add port or madeira.
Add raisins and almonds to taste and just before serving , add a stubby of beer.
Serve hot in glasses with some of  the raisins and almonds.

similar.name: Mulled Wine That Warms Your Soul
Mulled Wine That Warms Your Soul

name: Limorancia
directions: Use an orange vodka! (i use stoli orange) Shake ingredients in iced filled shaker.shake until tin turns cold, pour into a frozen martini glass make an orange and lemon twist over drink, drop twists in drink and serve.

name: Limoncello Martini
directions: Shake with ice and pour into a martini glass.
Garnish with a lemon twist.

name: Russian Lemonade
directions: Shake with crushed ice and strain into a tall HighBall.
Garnish with mint leaf (voluntarly).

name: Mulled Wine That Warms Your Soul
directions: Pour wine into a sauce pan with the wine, sugar and spices.
Bring to the boil,  then simmer for 5 minutes. Turn off the heat and leave to macerate for at least 30 minutes before serving.
Add the vodka, a slice of orange and serve.

similar.name: Mulled Wine That Warms Your Soul

name: Russian  Tea
directions: mix the above.
store in an air tight container.
to use-place 1-2 T of mix in a mug. Pour hot water over and enjoy.

name: Russian Mint Tea
directions: Tie tea leaves, cherries & mint leaves into a muslin or cheesecloth bag.
Bring 2 qts water to a brisk boil then add tea bag.
Remove from heat & allow to steep for 20 minutes
Serve and add sugar or honey as desired.

name: Best Sangria Ever
directions: Juice all but one of the oranges and limes.
Mix juice, wine, triple sec and apricot brandy in a pitcher.
Slice remaining orange and lime into thin slices and drop into pitcher.
Put ice into red wine glass or brandy snifter.
Pour glass 2/3 full of sangria and top remaining 1/3 of glass with club soda.
Stir and enjoy!

name: Apple Cinnamon Glögi
directions: Combine apple juice, cider, vanilla bean and cinnamon stick in a saucepan and bring to boil.
Turn off the heat, cover, and let stand for a few hours.
Remove vanilla bean and cinnamon stick.
Add whiskey, and reheat (do not boil).
Add raisins and blanched almonds to each glass and pour the hot drink into the glasses. Serve immediately.

name: Red Grape Glögi
directions: Combine the juice and the spices in a saucepan, bring to boil, and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add brown sugar to taste. Pour into glasses, garnish with slivered almonds and serve hot.

name: Apple White Wine Glögi (Mulled Wine)
directions: Combine apple juice with honey and spices in a saucepan.
Bring to boil and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add wine and reheat gently, do not boil!
Pour into glasses and garnish with sultanas and blanched almonds.

similar.name: Glögi Spice Mixture for White Wine
Mulled Wine That Warms Your Soul

name: Mulled White Wine
directions: Combine all ingredients in a saucepan and heat gently (do not boil!).
Sieve and serve hot.

similar.name: Glögi Spice Mixture for White Wine
Glögi Spice Mixture for Red Wine

name: Apple Glögi
directions: Combine ingredients in a saucepan and gently heat to boil. Simmer gently, covered, for 5-10 minutes.
Remove from heat, transfer into a bottle and refrigerate overnight.
When ready to serve, sieve the mixture and gently reheat to boiling point.

name: Glögi Spice Mixture for White Wine
directions: Combine all ingredients in a saucepan, bring to boil and allow to simmer for 15 minutes.
Sieve the mixture and store in the fridge.
Use 100-200 ml of the spice mixture per one (750 ml) bottle of white wine (or equal amount of fruit or berry juice): mix and heat gently.

similar.name: Glögi Spice Mixture for Red Wine

name: Glögi Spice Mixture for Red Wine
directions: Combine ingredients in a saucepan and simmer for an hour.
Allow to cool and sieve.
Store in a bottle in the fridge.
Use 100 ml or more per one (750 ml) bottle of red wine (or equal amount of red grape or black currant juice): mix and heat gently. Sweeten with brown sugar, if you prefer.

similar.name: Glögi Spice Mixture for White Wine

name: Russian Spring
directions: Shake together with ice the first 3 ingredients.
Strain into highball glass half filled with cubed ice. Add soda water and garnish with a sprig of mint.

name: Vanilla Twist Cocktail
directions: Shake all ingredients with ice in a shaker, strain into an ice-filled tumbler & top with soda water if desired.

name: Pink Vanilla Cocktail
directions: Place ingredients in a shaker with ice, & shake to combine.
Strain into a martini glass and garnish with a lime wedge.

name: Galliano Breeze Cocktail
directions: Shake all ingredients with ice in a cocktail shaker, strain into a glass.

name: Superbo Cocktail
directions: Build over ice in a highball glass, top with ginger beer.

name: Nuovo Cocktail
directions: Build over ice in a highball glass.
Garnish with a star anise & 2 blueberries.

name: Dk's Swedish Glögg
directions: Stir together Aquavit and Wine with sugar into a large pot.  Wrap remaining ingredients in cheesecloth, place in pot with low heat for a couple of hours (or crockpot all day).  Serve hot and discard cheesecloth wrapped ingredients.
Variation 1:  For a milder version and more servings, you can double the wine.
Variation 2:  Vodka can be used in the place of Aquavit (Aqvavit) but it will not be as flavorful.

name: Mulled Wine That Warms Your Soul
directions: Pour wine into a sauce pan with the wine, sugar and spices.
Bring to the boil,  then simmer for 5 minutes. Turn off the heat and leave to macerate for at least 30 minutes before serving.
Add the vodka, a slice of orange and serve.

similar.name: Mulled Wine That Warms Your Soul

name: The Very Best Sangria
directions: In a pitcher.
squeeze 1/2 lemon  (Slice other 1/2 for presentation).
squeeze 1/2  of an orange (Slice the other 1/2 for presentation) Or use about 3 Tbsp of orange juice.
add cinnamon and sugar and stir.
add booze
fill pitcher with ice.
add lemon and orange slices
stir again
Serve and enjoy.

name: Glühwein (Mulled Wine) - Debrosses Restaurant, Berlin
directions: Combine wine, sugar, raisins, almonds, anisette, cinnamon stick, cloves, lemon peel, and orange peel in heavy large saucepan.
Scrape in seeds from vanilla bean and then add bean.
Bring to a boil, stirring until sugar dissolves. Reduce heat and simmer 15 minutes to blend flavors.
(Can be made 1 day ahead. Cool, cover, and chill. Bring to simmer before serving.).

name: Lavender Herbal Tea Blend
directions: Rinse the inside of the teapot with hot water.
Place the loose herbs into the teapot, or into a tea ball. Add boiling water to the pot and steep for 5 minutes; strain.
Serve in warmed china cups, with honey to taste. Makes two cups of tea.
Note: If you would like a stronger tea, add a black tea bag or loose tea of your choice and you can add another cup of water to make 3 cups of tea.

name: Sassy Sangria Rose
directions: Combine the wine, triple sec and lemonade concentrate in a pitcher.
Chill well, for at least one hour.
Garnish with the citrus before serving.
Serve in large wine glasses.

name: Littlemafia's Hot Wine
directions: Pour the wine into a cover pot and add the sugar,cinnamon and cloves.
When is starts to boil turn off the heat.
Remove the cinnamon and cloves and serve hot,.

similar.name: Mulled Wine That Warms Your Soul
Mulled Wine That Warms Your Soul

name: Kazakh Chai (Kazakh Tea)
directions: Combine the water, tea, cardamon, and fennel seeds in a large saucepan and simmer over low heat for 3 minute
Add the milk and 2 min later  strain the tea into cups.
Add sugar/honey.

name: Frangelico Caipiroska
directions: Muddle the lime & sugar in the bottom of a tumbler glass.
Add all other ingredients and fill with ice, stir gentyly before serving.

name: Dr. Johnson's Second Choice
directions: Heat the wine, sugar and cloves together until almost boiling. Pour in the boiling water.
Add the Triple Sec and the brandy. Pour into heat-proof glasses and sprinkle with nutmeg.

name: Glühwein (German Mulled Wine)
directions: Heat the red wine to a low simmer. Add all other ingredients to the red wine. Heat for approximately 5 minutes then remove from the heat.
Cover and allow to infuse for 1 hour.
Reheat and pass through a sieve before serving.

name: English Spiced Wine
directions: Place all ingredients in a pot and slowly bring to a simmer over a low flame. Do not boil.
Cover and let steep on a very low flame for about 15 minutes.
Strain, ladle into mugs and serve.

similar.name: Mulled Wine That Warms Your Soul
Mulled Wine That Warms Your Soul
Glögi Spice Mixture for Red Wine

name: Greek Coffee
directions: Fill a heat-proof, preferably clear cup with coffee. Stir in apricot brandy and Metaxa.
Top with whipped cream.

name: Auringonlasku
directions: In a bar mixing glass filled with ice, stir in vodka and brandy.
Strain into a highball glass filled with ice.  Top off with orange juice.
Pour grenadine in slowly.  Garnish with an orange slice and a swizzle stick.

name: German Coffee
directions: Pour the coffee and Cherry brandy into a specialty coffee cup, and add the sugar to sweeten.
Top with whipped cream and an optional cherry.

name: Glühwein (Non-Alcoholic Version)
directions: Slowly heat the apple juice and tea in a pan.
Squeeze the juice from the lemon and orange, keeping the peels.
Add the lemon and orange juices, sugar, peels, and spices to the pan and heat without boiling (about 15-20 minutes).
Carefully strain the mixture through a sieve and serve.

name: Jellybean Cocktail
directions: Mix ouzo, ice, lemonade and blue curacao together.
Pour into a glass.

name: Ampiainen
directions: Put some ice cubes into a tall glass, add vodka and banana liqueur and top it with ginger ale. Stir well, garnish with a red cherry and serve.

name: Greek Mojito
directions: Muddle (or mush) the sugar, some soda water, lemon juice, lime wedge and Mint in a low or highball glass. Fill with crushed Ice and Metaxa and fill with soda water - stir it up and tip'er back!

name: "007" Martini
directions: Rinse martini glass with extra dry vermouth.
Add vodka, gin, and Lillet Blanc to a cocktail shaker filled with crushed ice.
Shake vigorously.
Pour into prepared martini glass.

name: London Lemonade Martini
directions: Stir or shake all ingredients together and pour into martini glass.
Garnish with lemon twist.

name: London Sun Martini
directions: Stir or shake all ingredients together and pour into martini glass.
Garnish with lemon twist.

name: Moneypenney (Martini)
directions: Add vodka, raspberry liqueur, and cranberry juice to a cocktail shaker filled with ice.
Shake and strain into a martini glass.
Garnish with the cherry.

name: Iced Russian Vodka
directions: Find an empty 1/2 gallon milk or juice carton (the cardboard kind) and rinse out. Cut the top of the carton off (about 3/4 of the way up).
Place the bottle of vodka inside the milk carton and fill 3/4 full with water. Carefully place in the freezer and allow 24-48 hours to freeze.
To remove the carton, dip it into a sink filled with warm water. After a few seconds, the cardboard should tear away easily.
Place on a dish or platter and wrap with a nice linen napkin for easy handling. Serve in stemmed shot glasses.

name: Sangria Bites
directions: Pour the simple syrup into a small saucepan, sprinkle gelatin overtop and stir to combine.
Let stand until gelatin softens, about 2 minutes.
Place this mixture over low heat and stir, gently splashing around the sides of the pan until all of the gelatin has dissolved. Do not boil.
Stir the wine and brandy into the gelatin mixture and stir slowly for 1 minute. Pour into an 8-inch  glass baking dish and refrigerate for 2 hours or until very firmly set.
Wash and dry the orange and remove 2 strips of rind using a vegetable peeler.
Use the blade of a knife to scrape away all the white pith on the rind then slice into very thin strands, each about 1⁄2 inch  long.
Cut the set jelly into 3⁄4-inch cubes.
Remove from the pan using a rubber spatula or an off-set palate knife. Garnish each cube with a few orange-peel slices before serving.

name: Kate's Ring Martini
directions: Shake and Strain.
Rim Martini Glass with Sugar (the "diamonds") and float 1 ounce of Mionetto Sparkling Prosecco.

name: Frangelico Chocolate Martini
directions: Shake ingredients in cocktail shaker with ice.
Strain ingredients into glass and garnish with cherry.

name: Americano
directions: Pour the spirits into a chilled Collins glass with ice cubes, stir and garnish with an orange slice or a twist of lemon peel.

name: Sgroppino
directions: Place the sorbet and vodka in a chilled champagne flute and stir gently. Top with Prosecco and garnish with freshly chopped mint and lemon twist.

name: Gluhwein
directions: Combine the ingredients in a pan and heat (don't boil!):  strain and.
serve.

name: St. Moritz Cocktail
directions: Grab a shot glass and add 3/4 oz of Black Raspberry Liqueur.
Next, carefully pour 3/4 oz of Cream over the back of a bar spoon to create a second layer.

name: Cafe Venitzio
directions: Grab your favorite coffee mug and add 3/4 oz of Amaretto, 3/4 oz of Brandy and 3/4 oz of Galliano.
Fill your cup with hot Coffee and serve.

name: Saint Moritz Cocktail Switzerland
directions: Grab a shot glass and add 3/4 oz of Black Raspberry Liqueur. Next, carefully pour 3/4 oz of Cream over the back of a bar spoon to create a second layer.

name: Swiss Cocktail
directions: Stir in mixing glass with ice & strain into cocktail glass.
Add lemon twist.

name: Champagne St Moritz
directions: Shake Gin, brandy and orange juice with ice.
Strain into two champagne flutes and top with chilled champagne.
Garnish with an orange slice and serve.

name: La Dolce Vita Cocktail
directions: Fill a collins glass with ice.
Pour in all of the ingredients, in order, stir briefly, and add the garnish.

name: Bombardino
directions: Place brandy and Advocaat in an Irish coffee mug.
Top up with hot espresso or hot coffee, give a stir and adjust to your taste.
Serve hot with whipped cream on top.

name: Honeycrisp Apple Sangria
directions: Peel, core and chop one of the apples. In a bowl, combine the Chardonnay, Schnapps, pisco and honey. Add the quartered orange, the lemon, lime, cinnamon sticks, the chopped apple, ginger and cloves and refrigerate at least 4 hours or overnight.
Strain the sangria into a pitcher. Peel, core and finely chop the remaining apple. Peel, section and chop the remaining orange. Mix the apple and orange in a bowl.

On a small plate, blend the sugar and cinnamon. Moisten the rims of wine glasses and dip in the cinnamon sugar to coat. Fill the glasses with ice. Pour in the sangria until three-quarters full. Top with a splash of club soda. Garnish with a spoonful of the chopped apple and orange and a mint sprig.

name: Caravella Limontini
directions: Place all ingredients into a martini shaker along with several cubes of ice.
Shake vigorously, strain into a martini glass and serve immediately.
Garnish with a lemon twist.

name: Sunset Sangria
directions: Combine sugar, rum, orange liqueur, fruits and cinnamon sticks in a large pitcher. Cover with 1 bottle of the Rioja wine and chill sangria for several hours.
To serve, spoon fruits into glasses. Pour in the sangria and top off with a splash of soda water.

name: Union Jack Cocktail
directions: Shake all ingredients with ice, strain into a cocktail glass, and serve.

name: Sparkling Limoncello Raspberry Cocktail
directions: On a small plate, stir together the sugar and grated lemon zest. Dip a clean finger in the lemon juice and moisten the rim of a Champagne flute or other wine glass. Dip the rim in the sugar mixture to coat.

In a small bowl or ramekin, using a cocktail muddler or the end of a wooden spoon, pulverize the raspberries with the limoncello. Transfer the puree to the sugar-rimmed glass. Place a spoonful of crushed ice in the glass, then pour in the sparkling wine. Garnish with the mint leaves or lemon zest strip and serve immediately.

name: Mulled Wine (Glogg)
directions: In a large stainless steel pot, combine all ingredients (except the liquor).
Place over medium heat and bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat to the lowest setting and let simmer for about 30 minutes or until the flavors have blended.
Strain and remove all solids.
Keep in fridge if necessary, reheat and add liquor before serving.

name: French Pear Martini
directions: Rim cocktail glass with sugar.
Shake liqueurs into cocktail shaker filled with ice.
Strain into cocktail glasses.

name: Sour Cherry Vodka
directions: Put the cherries into a 1-quart Mason jar, and cover with the sugar.
Seal the jar and shake it several times, until the cherries are well coated with sugar.
Add the vodka, reseal, and shake the jar a few more times to mix.
Set aside in a cool, dark place for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a fine sieve into a 4-cup glass measuring cup.
If desired, transfer the liqueur to a decanter.
The Sour Cherry Vodka should be ready to drink immediately and has a shelf life of 2 to 3 months.

name: Jagertee
directions: Heat your tea, rum, wine, orange juice, brandy, spices, and lemons in a pot and let them come to a gentle simmer for about 5 minutes.
Remove from heat.
Add sugar to taste and serve.

name: Moldau Cocktail
directions: Shake in iced cocktail shaker & strain.
Serve in a cocktail glass.
Add brandied cherry.

name: Yellow Plum Cocktail
directions: Shake in iced cocktail shaker & strain into a martini glass.

name: Grand Plum Cocktail
directions: Stir in iced cocktail shaker & strain into a martini glass.

name: Bavarian Mulled Wine
directions: Combine all the ingredients in a large pot and place over medium-low heat.
Heat the mixture for 60 minutes, making sure not to bring the mixture to a boil.
Once the flavors have melded together for 60 minutes, strain out the spices and reserve the citrus rounds for serving.
Serve the warmed wine with a round of lemon and a round of orange.

name: Sorrentino Cocktail
directions: Using a knife cut into the lemon peel removing a strip around the lemon at least 3 to 4 inches long.  Artfully twist the strip.
Place the thyme into the glass and muddle by gently pressing to release the flavor of the thyme.
Cut the oranges into thin round circles.
Place the oranges on the side of the glass with the ice in the middle to hold the orange slices against the glass.
Using a shot glass or jigger to measure add the Limoncello, Campari, & Vermouth.
Pour in the Club Soda almost to the rim of the glass.
Garnish with a sprig of thyme and the lemon twists.  Garnishing completes the look and the aroma of the cocktail as you place it close to your nose to take a drink.
Enjoy the Sorrentino Cocktail!

name: Randy's Sangria
directions: In a large vessel, combine all liquid ingredients and stir. Let rest for 5 -10 minutes, taste, add sugar if you would like more sweetness, and stir to dissolve. Chill several hours or overnight. Serve in glasses with ice.

name: Sangria!
directions: 1. In a large pitcher or bowl, mix together the brandy, lemon juice, lemonade concentrate, orange juice, red wine, triple sec, and sugar. Float slices of lemon, orange and lime, and maraschino cherries in the mixture. Refrigerate overnight for best flavor. For a fizzy sangria, add club soda just before serving.

My Note:  The brandy is the key to this recipe! My step-mother (from Madrid) showed me how to make this. Use Spanish Rioja wine. I go a bit heavier with El Presidente brandy and go a bit easier on the fruit juices since that was what I was taught. I can see where some would prefer a sweeter version but me, I like the juice from the fruit to add their signature flavors, not anything else. Make sure to let it sit for several hours before serving. A definite party-maker!

I made it for 50 servings & everyone absolutely loved it! No sugar & used gingerale instead of carbonated water; used rum, not brandy; used peach schnapps, not triple sec. Refrigerated all ingredients night before. Made it early that afternoon & sat container in an ice chest. Added cold gingerale just prior to serving. Sooooo good! Can't wait to have another party!

name: Mojito
directions: Place ice in beverage shaker then add in the rum, 8 broken up mint sprigs, lime juice and sugar. Shake well and serve over ice in a high ball glass. Top off each glass with a splash of club soda.
Garnish each with a slice of lime and a sprig of mint.

name: Russian Caravan
directions: Combine all together (in a shaker if you have it) and pour over ice in a highball glass.
Top with club soda. Enjoy.

name: Russian Tea With Fruit
directions: Put tea in a container that will preserve heat and pour 1 cup boiling water over it. Let it steep for 3 minutes.
Strain 1/4 cup tea into each tea cup. Fill with boiling water.
Put 1/4 of the sugar, lemon, and preserves in each cup to taste.
Stir and serve.

name: SPANISH SANGRIA
directions: In a large pitcher, combine all the ingredients except carbonated beverage and ice cubes; stir well. Cover with plastic wrap and transfer to the refrigerator for 24 to 48 hours.
Before serving, add 7Up® or any other carbonated beverage of choice and ice cubes; stir well and serve immediately.
